apiOrSchemaChanges: Result struct may gain an Unsupported bool field
Score() may now return Grade="N/A" with empty dimensions for TierGeneric units (new return shape)
scoreFromMetrics may no longer emit correctness=0.5 or testability=0.5 for unmeasured dimensions (keys absent from map)
failingTest: Tests in internal/engine/scorer_test.go that currently expect 0.5 defaults for unmeasured dimensions (will break when defaults are removed) and any tests that pass TierGeneric-language units through Score() expecting dimension scores.
fileMap: internal/engine/scorer.go — modify — add language-tier gate, complexity guard, remove 0.5 defaults, update doc comment, skip Unsupported in aggregates
internal/engine/scorer_test.go — modify — update tests expecting 0.5 defaults; add TierGeneric short-circuit, zero-complexity guard, and aggregate-skip test cases
fixApproach: Add a language-tier gate at the top of Score() so TierGeneric units short-circuit to Grade="N/A" with no dimension scores; guard the complexity-based maintainability rule in scoreFromMetrics to skip when complexity is an untrustworthy zero; remove the 0.5 default injections for correctness and testability so unmeasured dimensions are truly absent from the output map (matching the existing doc contract); and ensure any aggregate loop skips Unsupported units. Update/add tests to cover each new code path.
openQuestions: Does the result struct already have an Unsupported bool field, or must it be added?
What are the exact tier constant names in the language_tiers package (TierGeneric, TierNone, TierNative, etc.) and the full import path?
Does the language_tiers package expose a parser-capability boolean per tier, or should we infer it from tier identity alone?
Is there an existing aggregate loop in scorer.go that iterates units (which must skip Unsupported), or is aggregation handled elsewhere?
steps: Read internal/engine/scorer.go to understand Score() signature, scoreFromMetrics signature, the result struct fields, and all 0.5 default injection points.
Read internal/language_tiers/ package (tier constants, TierForLanguage signature, parser-capability indicators if any).
Read internal/engine/scorer_test.go to understand existing test patterns and which tests exercise Score/scoreFromMetrics.
In scorer.go, add the language_tiers import.

In Score(), add the pre-scoring gate: call TierForLanguage(unit.Language); if tier == TierGeneric, return a result with Grade="N/A", an Unsupported=true field on the struct (add the field if absent), and an empty/nil dimension map. Do NOT call scoreFromMetrics.

In scoreFromMetrics, guard the complexity ≤ 5 → 0.95 maintainability rule: if complexity == 0 AND the language tier lacks parser capability (TierGeneric / TierNone), omit the maintainability dimension entirely (no entry in the map).
Remove every injection of correctness=0.5 and testability=0.5 for unmeasured dimensions so those keys are absent from the output dimension map.
Update the Score() doc comment if removing the 0.5 defaults changes the described contract — ensure doc matches behaviour.
Ensure any aggregate calculation loop (if present in scorer.go) skips units where Unsupported == true.
Update existing tests in scorer_test.go that expect 0.5 defaults; add new test cases for TierGeneric short-circuit, zero-complexity guard, and aggregate skip.
Run go test ./... -count=1 and fix any failures.
summary: Guard Score() with a language-tier gate for TierGeneric → N/A, protect the complexity maintainability rule from firing on unmeasured zero values, and remove 0.5 dimension defaults so output matches the doc contract.
